To a stirred solution of benzaldehyde (530 mg, 5.00 mmol, 1.0 equiv) and piperidine (468 mg, 5.50 mmol, 1.1 equiv) in dichloroethane (15 mL) was added acetic acid (300 mg, 5.00 mmol, 1.0 equiv) at room temperature. After stirring for 30 min, sodium triacetoxyborohydride (1.59 g, 7.50 mmol, 1.5 equiv) was added in one portion. The reaction was stirred at room temperature for 16 h, then quenched with saturated NaHCO3 (15 mL). The aqueous layer was extracted with DCM (3 × 20 mL), and the combined organic layers were washed with brine (20 mL), dried over Na2SO4, filtered, and concentrated. The crude product was purified by flash chromatography on silica gel (DCM/MeOH, 95:5) to afford N-benzylpiperidine as a colorless oil (788 mg, 90%).
